title: panic: chown /home/snowdrop/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l: operation not permitted
body:
## Issue

OS: `Centos 7`
Linux kernel version: `3.10.0-1160.42.2.el7.x86_64`

The following error is reported :
```
[snowdrop@h01-121 poc]$ go run bud.go
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["storage.options.override_kernel_check"] from "/etc/containers/storage.conf".
ERRO[0000] error creating buildah storage !
panic: chown /home/snowdrop/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/l: operation not permitted
```
by go when `storage.getStore()` is called
```
	storeOptions, err := storage.DefaultStoreOptions(unshare.IsRootless(), unshare.GetRootlessUID())

	store, err := storage.GetStore(storeOptions)
	if err != nil {
		logrus.Error("error creating buildah storage !")
		panic(err)
	}
```

## Go Main example
```
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"github.com/containers/buildah"
	"github.com/containers/buildah/define"
	"github.com/containers/buildah/imagebuildah"
	"github.com/containers/storage"
	"github.com/containers/storage/pkg/unshare"
	rspec "github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/specs-go"
	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"io/ioutil"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"time"
)

func main() {
	ctx := context.TODO()
	dirname, err := os.UserHomeDir()
	if err != nil {
		logrus.Fatal( err )
	}
	tempdir, err := ioutil.TempDir(dirname, "buildah-poc-")
	//rootDir := filepath.Join(tempdir, "root")
	//runrootDir := filepath.Join(tempdir, "runroot")
	contextDir := filepath.Join(tempdir, "context")

	dateStamp := fmt.Sprintf("%d", time.Now().UnixNano())
	buildahImage := fmt.Sprintf("conformance-buildah:%s-%d", dateStamp, 1)

	var transientMounts []string

	output := &bytes.Buffer{}
	options := define.BuildOptions{
		ContextDirectory: contextDir,
		CommonBuildOpts:  &define.CommonBuildOptions{},
		NamespaceOptions: []define.NamespaceOption{{
			Name: string(rspec.NetworkNamespace),
			Host: true,
		}},
		TransientMounts:         transientMounts,
		Output:                  buildahImage,
		OutputFormat:            buildah.Dockerv2ImageManifest,
		Out:                     output,
		Err:                     output,
		Layers:                  true,
		NoCache:                 true,
		RemoveIntermediateCtrs:  true,
		ForceRmIntermediateCtrs: true,
	}

	// initialize storage for buildah
	storeOptions, err := storage.DefaultStoreOptions(unshare.IsRootless(), unshare.GetRootlessUID())

	store, err := storage.GetStore(storeOptions)
	if err != nil {
		logrus.Error("error creating buildah storage !")
		panic(err)
	}

	id, ref, err := imagebuildah.BuildDockerfiles(ctx, store, options, "Dockerfile")
	if err == nil && options.Manifest != "" {
		logrus.Debugf("manifest list id = %q, ref = %q", id, ref.String())
	}
}
```

comments.body: I have a feeling that containers/storage will no longer run on a kernel as old as is in RHEL7.
Is a root path needed as the error occurs within a non root path `panic: chown /home/vagrant/buildah-poc-3880267168/root/overlay/l: operation not permitted` ?

FYI: The `buildah bud` command works on the same machine and should be similar to what I try to

```
[vagrant@centos7 poc]$ buildah bud -f Dockerfile .
STEP 1: FROM alpine
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob a0d0a0d46f8b done
Copying config 14119a10ab done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: CMD ["echo", "Hello StackOverflow!"]
STEP 3: COMMIT
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e2eb06d8af82 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 5f70bf18a086 done
Copying config 30db644528 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
30db64452865a24a3295273e86d29400534f2e5caa551033229f0c349ae41da7
30db64452865a24a3295273e86d29400534f2e5caa551033229f0c349ae41da7
[vagrant@centos7 poc]$ 
```

Problem is fixed If I execute the code using `sudo`

```
sudo ./out/bud
WARN[0000] Failed to decode the keys ["storage.options.override_kernel_check"] from "/etc/containers/storage.conf". 
✔ docker.io/library/alpine:latest
```

Question: Can buildah be executed as non root to perform a `bud` ?

Yes. it enters the user namespace.

Try buildah unshare ./out/bud or podman unshare ./out/bud

The buildah CLI does this when it detects that it needs to (by calling `unshare.MaybeReexecUsingUserNamespace(false)` in `before()` in `main.go`), but the sample program does not.  The sample program also needs to be calling `buildah.InitReexec()` at the top of `main()`.
May I suggest that buildah then report a more user friendly message such as "Root privileged permission is needed when you execute buildah <CMD> or code as reported here" ?
This error is down in container storage.  @nalind shouldn't this have happened on the mount not on the chown?
@rhatdan the `idtools.MkdirAllAs(path.Join(home, linkDir))` call in `drivers/overlay.Init()` happens before we start testing for kernel features.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since this is not a buildah issue, but a vendoring of buildah, I don't see a way for us to fix this. So I am going to close this issue.  Reporter has figured out a way to make it work.
